title: Reference to Aristophanes in Platos The Symposium
display_title: Reference to Aristophanes in Platos The Symposium
wikitext:
[]: Reference to Aristophanes in Plato's The Symposium             Quote Post by Blacklightxxxx » Sun Jul 13, 2014 3:34 pm I was wondering about Eva (as I usually do since I have lots of time ) According To Greek Mythology…. The first humans were created with four arms, four legs and four eyes… They had two noses and two mouths and they terrified Zeus… He believed they had eminent powers and feared there would come a day when one would take his place as Ruler. To prevent such an incident from ever occurring, Zeus split each human in half and left them to wander aimlessly around the mortal world searching for their other half….. Their one true soul mate… Units 02, (apparently 08) and 13 have four eyes, Units 01-04 have two sets of nostrils, 13 has four hands and 05 four legs. (+ 03 developes another pair of hands when in contact with Bardiel) I was wondering if this was some sort of reference to their near-godlike being and difference from humans allthough they recemble giant humans biologically? (I'm very sory if this has been posted before, admins may do as they please if that's the case. )

[]:             Quote Post by peripateia » Wed Jul 16, 2014 2:54 am Black light, I don't think anyone has ever linked NGE to Plato's Symposium before! Here're my 2 cents: Aristophanes' speech on the origins of human 'incompleteness' (as you referenced) deals with the human longing for companionship and affection, stemming from Zeus's splitting of the original perfect human beings into separate halves. I liked Aristophanes' little story about human 'incompleteness'. But I think his way of looking at human frailty applies more to NGE's psychological case studies than to the design of the EVA units. The human instrumentality project may well resemble a return to the primordial state of being, to a state without loneliness or separation, i.e to that state of perfect humanity which Zeus came to fear. Meanwhile I believe Hayao Miyazaki once characterized the EVAs as "god warriors" barely restrained by their frail human controllers. I think Yamashita once said in designing the EVA units that his intention was to create the impression of a demonic entity just barely under the control of mankind. hp several theories have been proposed to explain the mythological influences behind the designs of the EVA units. I recall off the top of my head two theories: -the EVAs are designed to resemble Japanese demons, i.e Oni. -EVA units resemble the four beasts of the apocalypse, "the barons of hell" I really liked the thread here at Evageeks that discussed Unit 02's peculiar four eyes and how it related to mythological influences. Here is the link: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11111/Unit-02-Why-Four-Eyes/20/ and in that thread is a post by EvaMonkey on the 'four barons of hell' hypothesis: http://www.evamonkey.com/writings_faren01.p
